sections.paragraph: Ἀχαΐα, ας, ἡ [ἀχαῖοι, the term for Greeks in Hittite and Egyptian texts and Homer] Achaia, a Roman province governed by a proconsul 27 bc–15 ad and, after an interval as an imperial province, again under a proconsul beginning in 44 ad; it included the most important parts of Greece, including Boeotia, Attica, and the Peloponesus Ac 18:12; 1 Cor 16:15; 1 Th 1:7f.
